title: dalbergia caudata
intro: Dalbergia caudata, also known as the Philippine rosewood or Philippine mahogany, is a tropical tree species native to the Philippines. It is valued for its timber, which is used in furniture-making and construction, and also has medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. Herbal preparations of Dalbergia caudata include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, and its essential oil is also used in aromatherapy.
